categories.label: Standards & Specs
categories.description: For the spec-keepers and interop builders.

categories.label: Leaders & Changemakers
categories.description: For the ones who move everyone forward.

categories.label: Heart & Team
categories.description: For the glue that holds people together.

categories.label: Bold & Adventurous
categories.description: For the fearless and the first.

categories.label: Culture & Cool
categories.description: For taste, trends, and star power.

categories.label: Fun & Energy
categories.description: For the life of the party.

badges.title: Risktaker
badges.description: For the person who leans into the uncertain moment and moves first, even when the safer choice is right there on the table.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who takes the bold shot, tries the untested idea, or volunteers for the thing no one else will touch.

badges.title: Opportunist
badges.description: Reserved for the person who sees the opening three seconds before anyone else and quietly walks through it.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer with a knack for spotting the moment, the angle, or the offer that everyone else missed.

badges.title: Cool Cat
badges.description: For the person whose calm makes hard rooms easier and whose vibe is somehow always dialed in.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who stays effortlessly composed, keeps their cool under pressure, and just makes hanging out feel good.

badges.title: Trailblazer
badges.description: Reserved for the person who goes first, cuts the path, and turns around to wave everyone else through.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who pioneers a new approach, opens a new space, or tries the thing no one has tried yet.

badges.title: Influencer
badges.description: For the person whose opinions shape what the rest of the group ends up caring about.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose recommendations, taste, or perspective consistently changes what others try, buy, or believe.

badges.title: Connector
badges.description: Reserved for the person who somehow knows exactly who you should meet and makes the introduction before you finish the sentence.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who bridges people, teams, and worlds, and turns strangers into collaborators.

badges.title: Trendsetter
badges.description: For the person who was into it six months before it was a thing, and will be onto the next thing by the time you catch up.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose choices, style, or interests reliably show up in everyone else's feed a few months later.

badges.title: Organizer
badges.description: Reserved for the person who turns a vague group idea into an actual date, place, plan, and shared calendar invite.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who makes gatherings, trips, or projects actually happen because they took the reins.

badges.title: Moderator
badges.description: For the person who keeps the conversation fair, the tone kind, and the space usable for everyone in it.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who steadies heated moments, welcomes newcomers, and holds the group to its better self.

badges.title: Leader
badges.description: Reserved for the person others quietly look to when it's time to decide, commit, or move.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who steps up, takes responsibility, and makes the group better by leading it.

badges.title: Expert
badges.description: For the person you text when you need the actual answer, not the guess.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose deep knowledge in a subject has saved you time, money, or embarrassment.

badges.title: Aficionado
badges.description: Reserved for the person whose love for a niche runs deep enough to change how the rest of us appreciate it.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose obsessive fondness for a topic, craft, or scene has made you a little more curious about it too.

badges.title: Psychic
badges.description: For the person who calls the outcome before it happens and then pretends they didn't.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose predictions, hunches, or gut reads have been unsettlingly on the money.

badges.title: Magician
badges.description: Reserved for the person who pulls off the thing that shouldn't have been possible and won't quite explain how.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who conjures results, opportunities, or fixes that look suspiciously like sleight of hand.

badges.title: Charmer
badges.description: For the person who could probably convince a locked door to open on its own.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose warmth, wit, and easy way with people gets doors, upgrades, and second chances.

badges.title: Cowboy
badges.description: Reserved for the person who rides in, does the thing their own way, and rides back out before the rules catch up.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer with a rugged, improvisational streak who gets it done without asking permission.

badges.title: Perfectionist
badges.description: For the person who notices the one pixel that's off and cannot rest until it isn't.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose exacting standards quietly raise the quality of everything the group touches.

badges.title: Wanderer
badges.description: Reserved for the person whose latest location update is somehow always a place you had to look up.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose restless curiosity keeps them moving, exploring, and bringing back stories worth hearing.

badges.title: Team Player
badges.description: For the person who makes the group stronger just by being in it and never needs to be asked twice.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who shows up for shared goals, shares credit generously, and puts the team ahead of the spotlight.

badges.title: Star
badges.description: Reserved for the person the room notices without them having to try.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose talent, presence, or performance consistently steals the show in the best possible way.

badges.title: Party Animal
badges.description: For the person still going strong three hours after everyone said they were leaving.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose energy keeps a night alive well past the point most people tap out.

badges.title: Trouble Maker
badges.description: Reserved for the person whose bad ideas make the best stories later.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose mischievous streak reliably produces just enough chaos to make things memorable.

badges.title: Doer
badges.description: For the person who stops talking about it around the time everyone else is still forming opinions, and just does it.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who ships, executes, and reliably turns intention into finished work.

badges.title: Entertainer
badges.description: Reserved for the person who could make a delayed flight fun and probably has.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose jokes, stories, and general showmanship keep everyone else laughing.

badges.title: Connoisseur
badges.description: For the person whose taste is educated enough that you just trust the pick.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose refined judgment in a specific domain reliably points the rest of us toward the good stuff.

badges.title: Change Maker
badges.description: Reserved for the person who looks at how things are and quietly starts building how they should be.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose ideas and actions have measurably shifted the culture, process, or outcomes around them.

badges.title: Ambassador
badges.description: For the person who represents the group well wherever they go, without ever making it feel like a pitch.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who speaks for the community, brings new people in, and makes outsiders feel welcome.

badges.title: Catalyst
badges.description: Reserved for the person whose presence in a conversation makes something new actually happen.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who sparks the idea, the collaboration, or the momentum that gets things moving.

badges.title: Challenge Maker
badges.description: For the person who invents the dare, the game, or the goal that pulls everyone else up a level.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who dreams up the fun, ambitious, or slightly-too-hard challenge the group ends up bragging about.

badges.title: Challenger
badges.description: Reserved for the person who pushes the assumption, asks the harder question, and won't let a lazy answer pass.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose willingness to disagree, in good faith, makes the whole group's thinking sharper.

badges.title: Enabler
badges.description: For the person who quietly clears the path so someone else can do their best work.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who removes blockers, opens doors, and makes it possible for others to succeed.

badges.title: Enthusiast
badges.description: Reserved for the person whose genuine excitement makes you care about things you didn't know you could care about.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose contagious energy for a topic or project pulls others in.

badges.title: Hot Shot
badges.description: For the person on the streak, delivering the win, and making it look easier than it is.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose recent run of results, moves, or performances has been undeniably impressive.

badges.title: Informer
badges.description: Reserved for the person who reliably shows up with the article, the update, or the heads-up before you needed it.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who keeps the group in the loop with useful, well-timed information.

badges.title: Maven
badges.description: For the person whose accumulated know-how in a domain is basically a public utility at this point.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose depth, generosity with knowledge, and pattern recognition make them the go-to in their field.

badges.title: Maverick
badges.description: Reserved for the person who plays their own game and somehow keeps winning it.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose independent streak, unusual approach, or refusal to conform produces real results.

badges.title: Party Planner
badges.description: For the person who thinks in guest lists, playlists, and vibe curves, and always seems to nail all three.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who designs gatherings that people are still talking about weeks later.

badges.title: Promoter
badges.description: Reserved for the person whose enthusiastic sharing has quietly built more audiences than they'll ever admit.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who champions other people's work, shows up to the events, and gets the word out.

badges.title: Propagator
badges.description: For the person who takes a small idea and helps it grow roots in five other communities.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer who spreads useful ideas, practices, or projects far beyond their original audience.

badges.title: Sage
badges.description: Reserved for the person whose considered advice tends to age extremely well.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose wisdom, perspective, and thoughtful counsel have helped others make better decisions.

badges.title: Tastemaker
badges.description: For the person whose picks quietly define what the rest of the group ends up loving.
badges.criteria: Award this to the peer whose eye, ear, or palate consistently sets the standard for what's good around here.

badges.title: Vibe Curator
badges.description: Reserved for the community members who know exactly how to make a room feel better, warmer, stranger, or more alive.
badges.criteria: Award this to the person who brings the perfect music, mood, lighting, playlist, or general atmosphere to any gathering.

badges.title: Chaos Coordinator
badges.description: For the person who somehow turns confusion, missed messages, late arrivals, and half-formed plans into an actual functioning experience.
badges.criteria: Award this to the person who saves the day when the group chat becomes a disaster and everyone else has emotionally resigned.

badges.title: Hype Architect
badges.description: Reserved for the rare friend who makes other people feel braver, cooler, funnier, and more capable than they did five minutes ago.
badges.criteria: Award this to someone who consistently celebrates others, boosts confidence, and brings main-character energy to their peers.

badges.title: Lore Keeper
badges.description: For the community member who remembers every inside joke, legendary night, strange detail, and oddly important piece of group history.
badges.criteria: Award this to the person who preserves the stories, explains the references, and keeps the mythology of the group alive.

badges.title: Side Quest Specialist
badges.description: Reserved for the person who can turn a normal day into an unexpected adventure with one suspiciously casual suggestion.
badges.criteria: Award this to someone known for spontaneous detours, weird little missions, hidden gems, and unusually successful side quests.

badges.title: Quiet Genius
badges.description: For the person who may not always take the spotlight, but regularly drops the exact idea, fix, or observation everyone needed.
badges.criteria: Award this to the person whose thoughtful contributions, subtle brilliance, or perfectly timed insight make the whole team better.

badges.title: Community Helper
badges.description: Reserved for the community members who show up when someone needs a hand, a pointer, a fix, or just a little extra kindness.
badges.criteria: Award this to the person who helped another community member solve a problem, feel welcome, find their way, or get unstuck.

badges.title: Food Sniper
badges.description: Reserved for the community members with impeccable taste and a talent for discovering the best culinary spots.
badges.criteria: Award this to the person known for providing the ultimate restaurant recommendations or leading the team to the best local eats.

badges.title: Visual Storyteller
badges.description: For the community member who turns ideas, data, moments, and messy concepts into visuals that people can actually feel and understand.
badges.criteria: Award this to someone who contributes strong creative assets, presentation design, diagrams, images, brand moments, or visual explanations.

badges.title: Logic Master
badges.description: Reserved for the person who brings structure to complexity, catches the hidden flaw, and makes the whole system make sense.
badges.criteria: Award this to someone who contributes technical thinking, clear reasoning, elegant problem-solving, or the decisive “wait, actually…” that saves the project.

badges.title: Schema Whisperer
badges.description: Reserved for the community member who can stare into a tangled credential payload and somehow emerge with elegance, order, and valid JSON-LD.
badges.criteria: Award this to the person who helps make badge data cleaner, more interoperable, and less likely to summon ancient standards demons.

badges.title: Alignment Wizard
badges.description: For the person who can connect a badge to a skill, a skill to a framework, a framework to a job, and somehow make it all seem obvious.
badges.criteria: Award this to someone known for mapping competencies, standards, occupations, and learning outcomes into something humans can actually use.

badges.title: CLR Cartographer
badges.description: Reserved for the brave soul who helps communities navigate the wild territory between transcripts, credentials, skills, evidence, and learner records.
badges.criteria: Award this to the person who makes Comprehensive Learner Records feel less like a maze and more like a map.

badges.title: Verifiable Vibes
badges.description: For the community member who believes trust is beautiful, signatures are poetry, and verification should work before anyone starts the demo.
badges.criteria: Award this to someone who helps ensure badges can be issued, held, shared, checked, and trusted without theatrical hand-waving.

badges.title: Badge Goblin
badges.description: Reserved for the person who wants everything to be a badge, and is usually, annoyingly, kind of right.
badges.criteria: Award this to someone who keeps finding delightful, useful, or deeply unnecessary things that deserve to be credentialed.

badges.title: Metadata Mystic
badges.description: For the person who understands that the visible badge is only the charm on the necklace. The real magic is in the structured data underneath.
badges.criteria: Award this to someone who improves descriptions, evidence, criteria, tags, alignments, issuers, expiration, revocation, or any other sacred metadata field.

badges.title: Interop Diplomat
badges.description: Reserved for the patient negotiator who can bring platforms, vendors, wallets, registries, and institutions into the same room without causing a standards incident.
badges.criteria: Award this to the person who advances interoperability through grace, persistence, and the occasional heroic spreadsheet.

badges.title: Backpack Elder
badges.description: For the community member who remembers where the badges came from, why they mattered, and which conversations everyone keeps accidentally rediscovering.
badges.criteria: Award this to someone who carries the lore of Open Badges and helps newer people understand the movement, not just the spec.

badges.title: Issuer Therapist
badges.description: Reserved for the person who gently helps organizations admit that their badge criteria are unclear, their evidence is vibes, and their taxonomy needs love.
badges.criteria: Award this to someone who helps issuers design better badges with clearer value, stronger evidence, and fewer existential credentialing crises.

badges.title: Spec Gremlin
badges.description: For the person who reads the implementation notes, finds the edge case, asks the terrifying question, and saves everyone six months of pain.
badges.criteria: Award this to someone who catches ambiguity, breaks assumptions, stress-tests the standard, or says, “Technically…” and is unfortunately correct.
